hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W, F, Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L, I, V) will reduce off-target (SSTR3/5) binding by avoiding unfavorable electrostatic interactions in conserved pockets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by optimizing non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target interactions. This hypothesis is supported by the observed Δmargin values, but further refinement is needed to push Δmargin significantly above native SST-14.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve SSTR2 selectivity, we must disrupt conserved-pocket contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) that are likely responsible for off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions in off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 without compromising the native pharmacophore or structural integrity.
